questions.question: Consider a bank account with two processes, P1 and P2. P1 needs to access and update the account balance, while P2 needs to deposit money into the account. The critical section is updating the balance and the account is protected by a mutex. If P1 and P2 are executed simultaneously, what is the minimum guarantee on the order of operations?

An operating system has a single CPU and two processes, P1 and P2. The critical section is executing a time-critical task, and the processes are protected by a mutex. If P1 and P2 are executed one after the other, but with a 50% chance of being executed in either order, what is the minimum guarantee on the number of times the critical section is executed?

A bank account system has multiple processes accessing and updating the account balance, and is protected by a mutex. If there are n processes and n-1 mutexes, what is the minimum guarantee on the order of operations when all processes access the critical section simultaneously?
